evals.prompt: Create a simple Python script that prints 'Hello World'. Use the loop execution pattern.
evals.expected_output: A working hello_world.py file created in the project, verified and tested.
assertions: hello_world.py file exists in the project
The script runs successfully without errors
All 4 phases (plan, action, verify, test) were executed

evals.prompt: Refactor the following bad code to use proper error handling: def divide(a,b): return a/b
evals.expected_output: Refactored divide function with ZeroDivisionError handling and type checks.
assertions: Function includes type validation for inputs
Verify phase caught potential edge cases

evals.prompt: Fix the bug in this broken JavaScript: const x = null; console.log(x.y)
evals.expected_output: Fixed code with null check, verified no runtime errors.
assertions: Code includes null/undefined check before accessing property
Verify phase identified the null dereference risk
